items.title: Ukraine, 9 countries form ballistic missile defense coalition
items.description: Ukraine and nine European countries launched a coalition to protect Europe from ballistic missiles, drawing on Kyiv’s wartime experience.

items.title: Trump says to deliver 'speech to the nation' Thursday as Iran tensions escalate
items.description: US President Donald Trump said on Monday he will deliver a prime-time address to the nation on Thursday, without disclosing the topic. The announcement came hours after Trump said he would reimpose a US naval blockade on Iran and impose a 20% fee on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Nigeria, Hong Kong sign double taxation treaty to boost trade, investment
items.description: The agreement is expected to eliminate double taxation on income earned in both jurisdictions, strengthen tax cooperation and encourage greater cross-border investment. The post Nigeria, Hong Kong sign double taxation treaty to boost trade, investment appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Health fears as frail Indian activist on hunger strike loses 8.2kg
items.description: The environmental activist has been on a hunger strike for 16 days and is getting visibly weaker.

items.title: Pentagon pauses cyber audit rule blamed for supplier exits
Oil prices hit 1-month high as US-Iran attacks dim Strait of Hormuz outlook
items.description: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel amid renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran.

items.title: How people’s genes are revealing why some languages are so unique
items.description: A new study measured how different neighbouring languages were from one another. The authors say that linguistic hotspots may offer glimpses into how human languages became more varied before large migrations and population movements reshaped much of the world

items.title: Alleged killer's recorded prison calls played to murder trial
items.description: Recorded prison phone calls — in which the former partner of a woman confronts her alleged murderer — have been played in South Australia's Supreme Court.

items.title: South Korean court suspends FTC designation Coupang founder Kim Bom as controlling entity
Gas queues grow as Ukraine targets Russia's fuel supply
items.description: Attacks by Ukraine have triggered a fuel crisis in Russia. Reports from across Russia show how residents are dealing with the disruptions in their daily lives.

items.title: What would it mean for Ukraine if it actually starts producing Patriot missiles?
items.description: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.

items.title: India's LTM expects AI revenue to outpace traditional services, CEO says
World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
items.description: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

items.title: US military releases video of its latest strikes on Iran
items.description: US Central Command has released video showing its latest wave of attacks reportedly hitting Iran's mainland and islands.

items.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iran in Strait of Hormuz and will charge ships for safe passage
items.description: Trump said on social media that Iranian ships will no longer be able to travel through the Strait and America would charge a 20% toll on eligible cargo, as the conflict with Iran has intensified after peace talks failed to deliver meaningful progress.

items.title: Death toll from a Bangkok music bar fire rises to 30, dozens remain in hospital
items.description: Officials say the death toll from a huge fire in a Bangkok music bar has increased to 30

items.title: Person killed in ICE-involved shooting in Maine
items.description: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents were involved in a fatal shooting in Biddeford, according to Maine's House speaker. It comes days after ICE allegedly shot a man in Texas during a traffic stop.

items.title: UKTriple-murder suspect appears in South African court as extradition process begins
items.description: Authorities have also launched an investigation into how Tshuma allegedly obtained the unlicensed firearm, while police say there is no evidence he was harboured after arriving in South Africa.

items.title: Suns secure key signing, Bulldogs lose All-Australian defender
items.description: Shunning big-money offers from rival clubs, Jed Walter said the lure of achieving AFL glory with his childhood club made re-signing a "pretty easy decision".

items.title: US judge voids Trump's 'improper' $1.8 billion settlement with IRS
items.description: A US judge on Monday voided a settlement between President Donald Trump and the Internal Revenue Service over his leaked tax returns, ruling the lawsuit was filed for an "improper purpose". The agreement had granted Trump, his family and business broad protections from tax audits following their $10 billion lawsuit.

items.title: Petroleum Ministry responds to The Hindu editorial: ‘Higher Russian oil imports part of deliberate strategy’
items.description: The Ministry of Petroleum sought to address assertions made in an editorial by The Hindu regarding Russia’s share in India’s oil imports crossing 40% in May 2026.

items.title: Darline Graham Nordone, sister of Lindsey Graham, chosen to fulfill remainder of his U.S. Senate term
items.description: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster has named Darline Graham Nordone as her late brother Lindsey Graham’s temporary replacement in the U.S. Senate

items.title: Children were beaten, men chained — Rescued Oyo principal recounts kidnappers’ brutality
items.description: "The youngest of them, Waliya and Salam, received the worst beating. They would cover their mouths with clothes, tie them and beat them severely," the principal said. The post Children were beaten, men chained — Rescued Oyo principal recounts kidnappers’ brutality appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: 6,009 called to the Nigerian Bar, Body of Benchers cautions against misuse of ‘Barrister’
items.description: The chairperson of the Body of Benchers, Albert Akpomudje, cautioned against the improper use of 'Barrister' as a prefix before names, noting that it is not a professional title to be used in that manner. The post 6,009 called to the Nigerian Bar, Body of Benchers cautions against misuse of ‘Barrister’ appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Identity of Sydney daycare worker facing 329 child abuse offences revealed

items.description: The actor built up a long string of screen credits over a five-decade career.

items.title: Nine foreign nationals, including French woman, among 13 victims of Spain's deadly wildfire
items.description: At least thirteen people, including five Britons, three Belgians and one national each from France and Spain, were confirmed killed in one of Spain's deadliest wildfires in recent years, authorities said Monday. The blaze in the southeastern province of Almeria near the Mediterranean coast destroyed rural settlements and forced investigators to seek genetic information from suspected victims' relatives for identification.

items.title: Hungary: Lawmakers pass law to oust Orban ally president
items.description: The Hungarian parliament continued its attempts to leave the Viktor Orban era behind the country by moving to oust President Tamas Sulyok.

items.title: RSF chief Mohamed Hamdan Daglo sentenced to death in absentia
items.description: A court in Sudan's army-controlled city of Port Sudan on Sunday sentenced paramilitary leader Mohamed Hamdan Daglo and 15 others to death in absentia over charges of killing a regional governor and war crimes in Darfur, state media reported.

items.title: Germany set to curb its Freedom of Information Act
items.description: The German government wants to amend the Freedom of Information Act. Critics fear the changes will water down the government's obligation to disclose information.

items.title: Watch: Royal welcome for Norway: 100,000 fans celebrate world cup heroes
items.description: Norway received a breathtaking heroes’ welcome as more than 100,000 fans packed the streets of Oslo after the team’s historic FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign

items.description: ‌The ⁠arson attack is being treated as ⁠an antisemitic hate crime, according to officials.

items.title: New head of Arab League calls on Iran to cease attacks on Arab states
items.description: The Arab League’s newly installed Secretary General called on Iran on Monday to stop its attacks on US-aligned Arab States.

items.title: Record El Niño threatens to unleash floods across East Africa and Asia
items.description: Aid groups warn a record-strength El Niño could unleash flooding and hunger from Somalia to Pakistan.

items.title: Man charged with murder over death in Victoria's east
items.description: A 43-year-old man has been charged with murder following the discovery of the body of a 58-year-old Morwell man in Victoria's east at the weekend.

items.title: Subaru to recall over 541,000 US vehicles over incorrect label
Jurassic Park, The Piano, The Tudors... New Zealand actor Sam Neill dies at 78
items.description: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, known for his roles in "Jurassic Park" and "The Piano", has died aged 78. Neill had announced in April that he was cancer-free after undergoing treatment for stage-three ​blood cancer. Tributes are flowing in for the star, including from New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon who said Neill was "one of the greats". Story by Carys Garland.

items.title: World Cup stickers traded en masse in Mexico City as part of world record attempt
items.description: Thousands of people gathered Sunday in Mexico City's Zócalo to swap 2026 World Cup soccer stickers as part of a Guinness World Record attempt

items.title: Commentary: SK Hynix is a golden goose. It has to lay more eggs
items.description: The chipmaker’s blockbuster US listing signals South Korea’s arrival on the global economic stage, but retail investors should tread carefully, says Shuli Ren for Bloomberg Opinion.

items.title: Uganda: Court clears way for Besigye treason trial
items.description: The High Court has dismissed an application by Dr. Kizza Besigye and Hajji Obeid Lutale seeking to terminate treason proceedings, clearing the way for their trial to begin on July 13, 2026. The court ruled the application lacked merit and was intended to delay the criminal case.

items.title: How Kenyan volunteers hunt polio’s hidden trail
items.description: Community health volunteers travel across remote northern Kenya to detect poliovirus before it can spread.

items.title: Red Sea: a hinge between West Asia and Horn of Africa
items.description: Wars and rivalries across West Asia and the Horn of Africa are increasingly becoming interconnected; as the Gaza conflict spills into the Red Sea through Houthi attacks, Sudan’s war fuels competition among Gulf states and Ethiopia seeks greater maritime access, major powers are expanding their military, diplomatic and economic presence along this critical corridor linking two volatile regions

items.title: $10m worth of illegal tobacco, vapes seized by Tasmanian authorities last financial year
items.description: Tasmanian authorities seized five times as many cigarettes and three times as many vapes in the last financial year as they did the year prior. Enforcement agencies have been given greater powers, including the ability to shut down stores selling illegal tobacco.

items.title: Court rejects Ganduje’s request to compel PREMIUM TIMES reporter to disclose sources on port ownership report
items.description: Justice Simon Amobeda of the Federal High Court in Kano ruled that while the court possesses the authority to subpoena a journalist to testify, the reporter retains the right to protect their sources unless those sources are directly linked to the case. The post Court rejects Ganduje’s request to compel PREMIUM TIMES reporter to disclose sources on port ownership report appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

Bastille Day: France holds parade with Ukraine in focus
items.description: The celebrations features troops and warplanes from around Europe in a show of support for Ukraine, as it fends off Russia's ongoing invasion. It is Emmanuel Macron's last Bastille Day as France's president.

items.title: Ukraine strikes halt shipping on key wheat route as Russian fuel crisis deepens
items.description: Ukrainian drone strikes have brought shipping in the Sea of Azov to a near standstill, disrupting a corridor that carries up to a quarter of Russia's wheat exports and sending tremors through global grain and oil markets. They have also knocked out a significant share of Russia's refining capacity, prompting Moscow to ban diesel exports until the end of July and further unsettling global oil markets. In Russian-occupied Crimea, petrol prices have reached a record high.

items.title: Spy agency warns active Russian hackers targeting Australian industry
items.description: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

items.title: 10 years of Pokémon Go and the millions still trying to catch 'em all

items.title: India, Japan review defence ties, prepare ground for 2+2 dialogue later this year

items.title: Watch live: France's Bastille Day parade to showcase European support for Ukraine
items.description: France's annual July 14 military parade will feature aircraft from 11 European countries and around 500 troops from the Coalition of the Willing allies that have pledged to help with Ukraine’s post-war security in a showcase of support for Kyiv. The event will also mark President Emmanuel Macron’s last Bastille Day in his role. Watch the parade on FRANCE 24 from 10am Paris time by clicking on our liveplayer.

The hits were designed to prevent an Iranian plane, carrying a Houthis delegation, from landing. Story by Ellen Gainsford.

items.title: French police suspect arson in Fontainebleau forest near Paris
items.description: Firefighters continue to grapple with wildfires in the forest of Fontainebleau, 70km south of Paris, which have already burned through more than 1900 hectares. Police are now considering the possibility that the fires were started deliberately. Their suspicions were raised by the fact several fires broke out in close proximity to one another. Two arrests have already been made in connection to the fires. The latest with FRANCE 24 journalist Elitsa Gadeva.

items.description: After US strikes on Iran, Iran has targeted US allies in the Middle East. Even if Iran isn't targeting the governments or populations of its neighbors, Gulf countries have much to lose as fighting escalates.

items.description: Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed that Iranians would continue to avenge the killing of his father - late Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei - and of other top officials killed during the war triggered by the U.S and Israeli strikes in Iran on February 28.

items.title: Bastille Day: France celebrates Ukraine and its allies with military parade
items.description: France celebrates its national holiday today, celebrating the taking of the Bastille in 1789 that kickstarted the French Revolution. As ever, it is being marked by a massive military parade. The focus this year is France's rearmaments and support for Ukraine. 500 coalition troops, including some from Ukraine, will take part in the parade. France 24's Luke Shrago is on the ground at the Champs Elysees and tells us more about what we can expect from the parade.

items.title: 'We will never eliminate the risk of fire completely', expert says as wildfire rages south of Paris
items.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Monte Francis, Stefan Doerr, Director of the Centre for Wildfire Research at Swansea University, says that "we need to prepare the environment more to reduce the risk of fire but we will never eliminate it competely". France on Monday battled to contain a fire that scorched about 1,200 hectares in a forest south of Paris. Throughout the day, aircraft in the skies and firefighters on the ground were trying to douse the flames.

items.title: When London stank to high heaven

items.title: Two arrested as Fontainebleau forest near Paris ravaged by fire, prompting evacuations
items.description: France on Monday battled to contain a fire that scorched about 1,200 hectares in a forest south of Paris on Monday, as police arrested two people suspected of starting it. The fire erupted Sunday in the sprawling Fontainebleau forest, about 60 kilometres (40 miles) southeast of the capital, a former royal hunting estate that today is dotted with quiet villages. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris-Trent reports.

items.title: US strikes in Iran continue, Trump says reinstates blockade on Strait of Hormuz
items.description: US President Donald Trump says he is reinstating a blockade on Iran in the Strait of Hormuz and will charge ships for safe passage. The announcement comes after the two countries exchanged more fire following an Iranian attack on a ship in the waterway on Sunday. The UN's shipping agency says they have consistently opposed any nation charging a fee for passage through straits used ​for international navigation. Details by Morgan Ayre.

items.title: Spain wildfire: Firefighters gain ground as Almería evacuees return home
items.description: Firefighters gained the upper hand on the deadly Almería wildfire after days of battling the flames, as the blaze that killed 12 people and burned 66 square kilometres began to ease, allowing some of the nearly 1,500 evacuees to return home.

items.title: New era for Gibraltar with removal of 118-year-old border controls with Spain
items.description: It is hoped that free movement between the UK territory and Spain will provide an economic boost.

items.title: Argentina fans erupt as Messi's men reach World Cup semifinal
items.description: Argentina fans celebrate in Buenos Aires after a dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over ten-man Switzerland sends the defending champions into the World Cup semifinals.

items.title: Backed by Trump, Lindsey Graham’s sister Darline will replace him in Senate
items.description: South Carolina governor chooses Darline Graham Nordone to serve the rest of the late US senator's term until early 2027.

items.title: 'Falling apart': Major SA highways given lowest possible safety rating

items.description: The US has carried out a third consecutive night of strikes on Iran. Donald Trump says Washington is reinstating a naval blockade and warns Tehran will be “hit hard” as tensions continue to escalate. DW has more.

items.description: South African police arrested a man wanted by British authorities on suspicion of killing his wife and two young daughters in England after tracing him to Johannesburg, police said Friday.

items.title: A 60-year-old law could decide if Australia gets billions in AI investments
